sections.paragraph: pl. בֶּהָרֹות f. a spot in the skin, especially a white spot, which when it is lower than the rest of the skin, and has white hairs, is the symptom of the Oriental leprosy ( -Lev 13:24, -1823 , -2428 ); but if not, it is harmless, whether it be cicatrix, or it arise from burning, or it be vitiligo, verse 38 Lev 13:38, 39 Lev 13:39. Jahn (Archæol. i. § 215), incorrectly regards this word בַּהֶרֶת as being the special name of a certain disease, the λεύκη of Hippocrates; בֹּהַק differs from this as being the special name of a disease consisting of whitish spots.
